articles.headline: JetBlue Considers Selling Itself To A Major US Carrier, Weighs How Washington Will React
articles.blurb: JetBlue's exploration of a potential sale to rival airlines has sparked intense speculation and antitrust scrutiny.
articles.takeaway: JetBlue's sale consideration signals potential consolidation pressures in US aviation amid regulatory uncertainty about antitrust thresholds.

articles.headline: Up To 22-Hour Flights: United Airlines' 10 New Ultra-Long Routes In 2026
articles.blurb: Discover the airline's most extended nonstop and one-stop services, some of which are quite controversial.
articles.takeaway: Extended flight times will test crew fatigue management and passenger comfort economics, requiring operational redesigns around rest protocols and cabin services.

articles.headline: The US Airlines With The Most Spacious Premium Economy Seats In 2026
articles.blurb: This article will provide the reader with an in-depth look at the US airlines with the most spacious Premium Economy Seats In 2026.
articles.takeaway: US carriers are expanding premium economy pitch and width to compete for mid-tier passengers willing to pay 30-50% more than economy fares.

articles.headline: SMS Declaration of Compliance
articles.blurb: What the SMS Declaration of Compliance means for operators and what the FAA expects.
articles.takeaway: Operators must formally document their Safety Management System compliance to demonstrate proactive hazard identification and mitigation capabilities to FAA regulators.

articles.headline: NASA Sets Coverage for Artemis II Moon Mission
articles.blurb: Editor’s note: NASA will continuously update this Artemis II briefings and mission events page throughout prelaunch, launch, and mission activities. NASA will provide live coverage of prelaunch, launch, and mission events for the agency’s upcoming Artemis II crewed test flight around the Moon....
articles.takeaway: NASA's commitment to continuous public coverage of Artemis II signals increased transparency in lunar mission operations and may set new standards for government space program communications.

articles.headline: Artemis Moon Tree Dedicated in Honor of Mary W. Jackson
articles.blurb: On March 18, 2026, students, staff, and members of NASA’s Langley Research Center gathered at Mary W. Jackson Elementary School in Hampton to celebrate the dedication of a remarkable addition to the campus – an Artemis Moon Tree. Although formally dedicated on this day, the loblolly pine had...
articles.takeaway: NASA's Artemis Moon Tree program creates tangible educational connections between lunar exploration missions and STEM engagement at grassroots levels.

articles.headline: Why Emirates Doesn't Want The Boeing 737 MAX
articles.blurb: The airline has little interest in this popular narrowbody.
articles.takeaway: Emirates' fleet strategy prioritizes wide-body aircraft for high-capacity long-haul routes, making narrow-body jets like the 737 MAX incompatible with their Gulf hub model.

articles.headline: Safety Culture in Aviation SMS
articles.blurb: How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.
articles.takeaway: Psychological safety and blame-free reporting systems directly correlate with SMS effectiveness, requiring leadership commitment beyond policy documentation.

articles.headline: Southwest Airlines Passenger Blasts New Policy After Being Required To Buy An Extra Seat
articles.blurb: One passenger's ordeal at the airport in March 2026 raises questions about Southwest's 'Customer of Size' policy and its impact on travelers.
articles.takeaway: Southwest's enforcement of seating policies is creating public relations friction that could prompt broader industry scrutiny of size-based accommodation practices and liability exposure.

articles.headline: US House passes bill to clear way for overland supersonic flights
articles.blurb: The US House of Representatives passed legislation aimed at opening the skies to civil supersonic flight over land,…
articles.takeaway: Supersonic overland flights could unlock a substantial new market segment for premium business travel if noise-reduction technology proves commercially viable at scale.

articles.headline: Eve flies eVTOL prototype for Brazilian president in high-profile test milestone
articles.blurb: Eve Air Mobility flew its full-scale engineering prototype for Brazilian authorities, including President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva,…
articles.takeaway: Eve's demonstration to Brazil's president signals potential government backing that could accelerate regulatory approval and market entry for its eVTOL operations.

articles.headline: From the Flight Deck – Frederick Municipal Airport, MD (FDK)
articles.blurb: Frederick Municipal is a small general and corporate aviation airport located just east of the city of Frederick, Maryland. The traffic mix runs the spectrum from single engine training aircraft up...
articles.takeaway: Regional airports like Frederick are critical infrastructure for distributed general aviation training and corporate operations, diversifying flight activity beyond major commercial hubs.

articles.headline: How Many McDonnell Douglas MD-11 Aircraft Are In Service?
articles.blurb: The jet has mostly disappeared from our skies.
articles.takeaway: The MD-11's rapid retirement reflects how inefficient widebody designs face inevitable obsolescence in competitive aviation markets.

articles.headline: NASA Names Jennifer Lyons Acting Launch Services Program Manager
articles.blurb: NASA has selected Jennifer Lyons as acting program manager for the agency’s Launch Services Program (LSP) based at Kennedy Space Center in Florida. In this role, Lyons will lead NASA’s acquisition and management of domestic commercial launch services for science and robotic exploration missions...
articles.takeaway: NASA's LSP transition signals continued reliance on commercial launch providers for non-human spaceflight missions, reshaping aerospace procurement strategy.

articles.headline: BRINC Unveils Guardian, Launching the Next Era of Drone as First Responder
articles.blurb: Guardian delivers 24/7 operations, unmatched imaging, Starlink connectivity, and more to help public safety agencies
articles.takeaway: Guardian's Starlink integration enables remote drone operations beyond line-of-sight, expanding public safety agencies' response capabilities in areas lacking traditional communications infrastructure.

articles.headline: From the Flight Deck – Eastern Iowa Airport, IA (CID)
articles.blurb: Eastern Iowa Airport (CID) is a medium-sized multi-use airport located south of Cedar Rapids, Iowa. Traffic includes basic training aircraft up through air carrier aircraft. Flight schools often use...
articles.takeaway: Eastern Iowa Airport's mix of training and air carrier operations creates a unique operational environment that requires careful airspace management between student pilots and commercial traffic.

articles.headline: Office of Space Commerce releases mission authorization proposal
articles.blurb: The Office of Space Commerce has rolled out its proposal for a “light touch” approach to mission authorization, the latest attempt in an effort to regulate new commercial space applications.
articles.takeaway: The OSC's streamlined authorization framework aims to reduce regulatory barriers for commercial space operators while maintaining safety oversight, potentially accelerating market entry for new space ventures.

articles.headline: Flying Fox
articles.blurb: Cheap drones are no longer a compromise — they are becoming the strategy.The demand is
articles.takeaway: Budget drone models are now strategically positioned as primary solutions rather than alternatives, signaling a fundamental shift in how operators approach aerial platform economics.

articles.headline: Metrics

Service Level Agreement (SLA) Click here to view the Service Level Agreement The SLA provides information...
articles.takeaway: Understanding NSSC service metrics and SLAs helps aviation operators optimize procurement costs and service reliability through transparent chargeback rates and defined performance indicators.

articles.headline: Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization
articles.blurb: Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.
articles.takeaway: Operational optimization across airlines, airports, and flight planning offers immediate capacity gains without infrastructure expansion, addressing both congestion and emissions before new technologies mature.
keywords: aviation; optimization; operations research; sustainability; environmental impacts

articles.headline: NAVI AI Launches Pilot Training Platform
articles.blurb: Navi AI has unveiled a new AI-driven platform designed to enhance pilot training by automating post-flight debriefs.
articles.takeaway: Automating post-flight debriefs with AI reduces instructor workload and enables real-time performance analytics, accelerating pilot proficiency development while lowering training costs.

articles.headline: Pilot Shares What It’s Like To Perform A Go-Around During Landing
articles.blurb: A pilot reveals the reality of go-arounds—why they happen, how they’re flown, and why abandoning a landing is often the safest decision.
articles.takeaway: Go-arounds are a normal safety procedure, not a failure, requiring immediate crew coordination and fuel/terrain management that pilots must practice regularly.

articles.headline: Textron Aviation To Showcase Full Range Of Aircraft At Sun ’n Fun
articles.blurb: Textron Aviation will display Cessna, Beechcraft, and Pipistrel aircraft together for the first time at Sun ’n Fun 2026.
articles.takeaway: Textron's unified display strategy signals an effort to cross-sell its diverse aircraft portfolio across general aviation, business, and emerging electric segments to a broad customer base.

articles.headline: Cirrus posts strong 2025 with 691 SR Series deliveries, 106 Vision Jets
articles.blurb: Cirrus Aircraft reported it delivered 691 SR Series piston aircraft and a record 106 Vision Jets in 2025,…
articles.takeaway: Vision Jet's 97% year-over-year growth signals accelerating market adoption of single-engine jets among personal aircraft buyers, reshaping general aviation economics.

articles.headline: Webb Captures Saturn in Infrared
articles.blurb: Saturn’s icy rings glow in this infrared view from NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope released on March 25, 2026. Combining this image with another that NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope captured – also released on the same day – provides scientists with a richer, more layered understanding of the gas...
articles.takeaway: Webb's infrared imaging of Saturn demonstrates advanced observational capabilities that push beyond visible-spectrum astronomy, offering aerospace engineers insights into thermal imaging and sensor technologies applicable to space exploration missions.

articles.headline: Study Unveils Redevelopment Concepts For Burke Lakefront Airport
articles.blurb: A new economic study outlines two potential mixed-use redevelopment plans for Burke Lakefront Airport, part of Mayor Justin Bibb’s effort to close the city-operated airport.
articles.takeaway: Municipal airports face pressure to transition from aviation use to mixed-use development, signaling potential closure trends for underperforming city-operated facilities.

articles.headline: Terran Orbital Introduces New Star Tracker Product Line at SATSHOW 2026
articles.blurb: IRVINE, Calif., March 25, 2026 — Terran Orbital Corporation, a Lockheed Martin company, today announced the introduction of its new star tracker product line at SATSHOW 2026, expanding the company’s […]
articles.takeaway: Terran Orbital's star tracker expansion leverages Lockheed Martin's resources to address growing demand for precision attitude determination systems in the increasingly congested satellite constellation market.

articles.headline: Spirit Airlines vs. Frontier Airlines: Comparing The 2 Ultra-Low-Cost Carriers' Seat Pitch And Carry-On Bag Restrictions
articles.blurb: The two airlines offer a similar experience, but have slight variations.
articles.takeaway: Both ULCCs employ aggressive cabin density strategies, but Frontier's slightly more generous policies may signal competitive differentiation as ultra-low-cost carriers seek operational sustainability.

articles.headline: NASA Webb, Hubble Share Most Comprehensive View of Saturn to Date
articles.blurb: NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope and Hubble Space Telescope have teamed up to capture new views of Saturn, revealing the planet in strikingly different ways. Observing in complementary wavelengths of light, the two space observatories provide scientists with a richer, more layered understanding of...
articles.takeaway: Multi-wavelength space telescope coordination demonstrates how complementary observational capabilities advance planetary science methodologies applicable to aerospace research.

articles.headline: Why Did Antonov Construct The An-225 With 6 Engines?
articles.blurb: A closer look at one of the coolest quirks of a legendary aircraft.
articles.takeaway: The An-225's six-engine configuration was necessary to generate sufficient thrust for lifting 250+ tons of cargo while maintaining adequate safety margins and performance at maximum takeoff weight.

articles.headline: 10 Boeing Jets With The Longest Range Flying Today
articles.blurb: Boeing’s longest-range jets reach over 8,500 NM, enabling ultra-long-haul flights.
articles.takeaway: Ultra-long-range Boeing aircraft exceeding 8,500 NM enable airlines to operate point-to-point routes previously requiring hub connections, reducing operational complexity and improving passenger experience on transcontinental routes.

articles.headline: NASA-JAXA’s XRISM Telescope Clocks Hot Wind of Galaxy M82
articles.blurb: For the first time, astronomers have directly measured the speed of superheated gas billowing from a cauldron of stellar activity at the heart of M82.
articles.takeaway: X-ray spectroscopy advances from space telescopes enable precise measurement of galactic phenomena that inform understanding of extreme astrophysical environments relevant to aerospace engineering.

articles.headline: FAA Clears Higher Takeoff Weights For Boeing 787-9, -10
articles.blurb: The FAA has approved higher takeoff weights for the Boeing 787-9 and 787-10, giving operators more flexibility to carry additional payload or extend range on long-haul routes.
articles.takeaway: Higher certified weights unlock competitive advantages for 787 operators on ultra-long-haul routes, potentially reducing load-balancing constraints and improving payload-range economics.

articles.headline: Press Release: ZIPAIR selects Axinom Stream for next-gen streaming
articles.blurb: ZIPAIR Tokyo Inc. (“ZIPAIR”) and Axinom today announced the airline’s selection of Axinom Stream to power its modern onboard streaming platform as part of its in-flight entertainment (IFE) offering. Built on Axinom Stream, the solution enables DRM-protected Hollywood content delivery to passengers’...
articles.takeaway: ZIPAIR's adoption of Axinom Stream demonstrates how budget carriers are modernizing IFE systems with cloud-based, DRM-protected content delivery to compete on passenger experience without legacy hardware infrastructure.

articles.headline: NASA Awards Astrophysics Postdoctoral Fellowships for 2026
articles.blurb: The highly competitive NASA Hubble Fellowship Program (NHFP) recently named 24 new fellows to its 2026 class. The NHFP enables outstanding postdoctoral scientists to pursue independent research in any area of NASA Astrophysics, using theory, observations, simulations, experimentation, or instrument...
articles.takeaway: NASA's highly selective astrophysics fellowship attracts top postdoctoral talent, strengthening the aerospace sector's research pipeline for advanced space observation and scientific innovation.

articles.headline: NASA-ISRO Satellite Captures Pacific Northwest Through Clouds
articles.blurb: Seattle and Portland, Oregon, are among the cloudiest cities in the United States. But that infamous cloud cover is no match for the U.S.-Indian Earth satellite NISAR (NASA-ISRO Synthetic Aperture Radar), which is designed to peer straight through clouds. Doing so allows scientists to study the...
articles.takeaway: NISAR's all-weather synthetic aperture radar capability enables persistent Earth observation in perpetually clouded regions, expanding data availability for climate and infrastructure monitoring regardless of weather conditions.
